evidence: The paper analyzes Gini importance for the decision tree and GBT models, interpreting comparative feature importance rankings: characteristics such as retirement and number of children became relatively more important at later follow-up intervals (3-6 and 6-9 years) than at 0-3 years. These magnitude and ranking claims lack corroboration via permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or conditional importance, leaving them vulnerable to impurity-importance bias which could misattribute interactions (captured by GBT) or magnify effects on skewed features (e.g., smoking: 67.7% vs 3.2% by sex).
